acceptedQuestions.subject: Economy
acceptedQuestions.prompt: Which of the following schemes are combined under the composite Central Sector Scheme of the 'Skill India Programme'? 1. Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY) 2. Pradhan Mantri National Apprenticeship Promotion Scheme (PM-NAPS) 3. Jan Shikshan Sansthan (JSS) 4. Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Grameen Kaushalya Yojana (DDU-GKY) Select the correct answer using the code given below:
acceptedQuestions.explanation: According to the source text, PMKVY 4.0, PM-NAPS, and the Jan Shikshan Sansthan (JSS) Scheme are combined under the composite Central Sector Scheme of the 'Skill India Programme'. While DDU-GKY is a skill development initiative, it is part of the National Rural Livelihood Mission (NRLM) and is not listed as a component of this specific composite Central Sector Scheme.
acceptedQuestions.takeaway: The Skill India Programme is an umbrella Central Sector Scheme comprising PMKVY, PM-NAPS, and JSS.
acceptedQuestions.evidenceExcerpt: Key Components: Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana 4.0 (PMKVY 4.0), Pradhan Mantri National Apprenticeship Promotion Scheme (PM-NAPS), Jan Shikshan Sansthan (JSS) Scheme are combined under the composite Central Sector Scheme of “Skill India Programme”.
acceptedQuestions.reviewNotes: This question tests the structural organization of government schemes, specifically distinguishing between the Skill India Programme and NRLM components.

acceptedQuestions.subject: Economy
acceptedQuestions.prompt: With reference to the National Skill Development Corporation (NSDC), consider the following statements: 1. It is a not-for-profit public limited company established under the Companies Act. 2. It operates as a Public Private Partnership (PPP) model under the Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
acceptedQuestions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: NSDC was established in 2008 as a not-for-profit public limited company under Section 25 of the Companies Act, 1956 (now Section 8 of the 2013 Act). Statement 2 is correct: It operates as a unique Public Private Partnership (PPP) model under the Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship (MSDE).
acceptedQuestions.takeaway: NSDC is a PPP-based not-for-profit entity that serves as the strategic implementation partner for skill development in India.
acceptedQuestions.evidenceExcerpt: Established: In 2008, as a not-for-profit public limited company under section 25 of the Companies Act, 1956... It operates as a unique Public Private Partnership (PPP) model under the MSDE.
acceptedQuestions.reviewNotes: The question tests the institutional framework and legal status of NSDC, which is a frequent area of focus in UPSC prelims.

acceptedQuestions.subject: Economy
acceptedQuestions.prompt: Consider the following statements regarding vocational training schemes in India: 1. The Jan Shikshan Sansthan (JSS) scheme specifically targets non-literates, neo-literates, and school drop-outs in rural regions. 2. The Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY) provides free short-duration skill training and certification to the youth. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
acceptedQuestions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The Jan Shikshan Sansthan (JSS) scheme is designed to provide vocational training to non-literates, neo-literates, and school drop-outs, particularly in rural areas. Statement 2 is correct: PMKVY (launched in 2015) provides free short-duration skill training and certification to youth to enhance their employability.
acceptedQuestions.takeaway: JSS focuses on the non-literate and rural drop-out population, while PMKVY is a broader youth-centric skill certification program.
acceptedQuestions.evidenceExcerpt: Jan Shikshan Sansthan (JSS): Provides vocational training to non-literates, neo-literates and school drop-outs in rural regions. Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (2015): Free short-duration skill training and skill certification to youth.
acceptedQuestions.reviewNotes: This question effectively differentiates between two major skill initiatives based on their target demographics as per the source text.

acceptedQuestions.subject: Economy
acceptedQuestions.prompt: With reference to the 'India Skills Accelerator' initiative, consider the following statements: 1. It is a public-private collaboration platform launched by the Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship (MSDE) in partnership with the World Economic Forum (WEF). 2. It focuses on aligning education with industry needs in sectors such as Artificial Intelligence, robotics, and clean energy. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
acceptedQuestions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The India Skills Accelerator was launched by the MSDE in collaboration with the World Economic Forum (WEF) as a public-private collaboration platform. Statement 2 is correct: Its key objectives include aligning education with industry needs, particularly in high-growth sectors like AI, robotics, advanced manufacturing, and clean energy.
acceptedQuestions.takeaway: The India Skills Accelerator is a strategic partnership between the Indian government and the WEF to prepare the workforce for Industry 4.0 technologies.
acceptedQuestions.evidenceExcerpt: The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship (MSDE), in collaboration with the World Economic Forum (WEF), launched “India Skills Accelerator”. Key Objectives: Enable agile career transitions... in sectors such as AI, robotics, advanced manufacturing, and clean energy.
acceptedQuestions.reviewNotes: The question accurately reflects the international collaboration and sectoral focus of the initiative mentioned in the text.

acceptedQuestions.subject: Economy
acceptedQuestions.prompt: Regarding the Pradhan Mantri National Apprenticeship Promotion Scheme (PM-NAPS), consider the following statements: 1. It provides financial incentives to establishments engaging apprentices under the Apprentices Act, 1961. 2. The scheme provides 25% of the stipend, up to Rs. 1,500 per month per apprentice, through Direct Benefit Transfer. 3. It is applicable to the youth in the age group of 18 to 45 years. Which of the statements given above are correct?
acceptedQuestions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: PM-NAPS aims to provide financial incentives to establishments engaging apprentices under the Apprentices Act, 1961. Statement 2 is correct: It provides 25% of the stipend, up to Rs. 1,500 per month per apprentice through Direct Benefit Transfer. Statement 3 is incorrect: The scheme targets the age group of 14 to 35 years, not 18 to 45 years.
acceptedQuestions.takeaway: PM-NAPS uses financial incentives and DBT to promote apprenticeship, targeting a specific age bracket of 14-35 years.
acceptedQuestions.evidenceExcerpt: PM National Apprenticeship Promotion Scheme (PM-NAPS): Aims to provide financial incentives to the establishments engaging apprentices under the Apprentices Act, 1961. Provides 25% of the stipend, up to Rs.1,500 per month... Age group: 14 to 35 years.
acceptedQuestions.reviewNotes: The question includes specific factual details (stipend percentage and age group) that are typical of UPSC's approach to government schemes.

acceptedQuestions.subject: Economy
acceptedQuestions.prompt: With reference to the PM-SETU initiative for the modernization of Industrial Training Institutes (ITIs), consider the following statements: 1. It follows a hub-and-spoke model, linking hub ITIs to multiple spoke ITIs. 2. It involves the creation of Special Purpose Vehicles (SPVs) with industry partners to introduce demand-driven courses. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
acceptedQuestions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: PM-SETU follows a hub-and-spoke model, with 200 hub ITIs linked to 800 spoke ITIs. Statement 2 is correct: One of its main components is setting up Special Purpose Vehicles (SPVs) with industry partners to revamp courses and introduce new, demand-driven ones.
acceptedQuestions.takeaway: PM-SETU modernizes ITIs through a hub-and-spoke implementation model and industry-led SPVs.
acceptedQuestions.evidenceExcerpt: Implementation: PM-SETU will follow a hub-and-spoke model, with 200 hub ITIs linked to 800 spoke ITIs. Main Components: Introduce new, demand-driven courses... Set up Special Purpose Vehicles (SPVs) with credible industry partners.
acceptedQuestions.reviewNotes: The question covers the implementation architecture and industry-linkage aspect of the PM-SETU initiative.

reviewerNotes: The selected questions cover the breadth of the 'Skill Development and Global Capability Centres' section, focusing on institutional frameworks (NSDC), scheme components (Skill India Programme), specific scheme details (PM-NAPS), target demographics (JSS vs DDU-GKY), international collaborations (India Skills Accelerator), and modernization models (PM-SETU). All questions are grounded in the provided text and follow UPSC's statement-based and multi-select formats.
